# Convergence B2 — touch layout SIZE goldens (left / right / portrait / portrait2).
# Generated by TouchLayoutGoldenTest from the REAL TouchLayoutMetrics that
# VirtualControls.kt's composables call. Phase D's C++ ui/touch_layout.* re-parses
# each line's inputs, recomputes the RHS, and byte-compares.
#
# format: <KIND> w=<px> h=<px> [d=<density>] => <name>=<value> ...
#         ints decimal, floats 0x+8 hex of binary32 bits (house convention),
#         densities decimal (all are exact in binary32, so they round-trip).
#
# ⚠️ SIZES ONLY — NOT POSITIONS. Where each button lands is computed by Compose
# (Column/Row + Arrangement.Center; Portrait2 uses Modifier.weight(1f)), not by any
# Kotlin this file can call. A port matching every line here can still stack the
# D-pad in the wrong order. That hole is known and accepted: a wrong arrangement is
# obvious on a phone, a wrong proportion on an unowned screen size is not.
#
# PORTRAIT carries no density: boxWidth/boxHeight are px and density never enters.
# The (339|340|341, 510) triple straddles the patternRatio branch — 340/510 is the
# exact tie, and `boxRatio < patternRatio` being strict sends it down the HEIGHT arm.

# LEFT box — L trigger, D-pad, SELECT.

# RIGHT box — R trigger, A, B, START.
# ⚠️ med=0.7X here where LEFT's is 1.0X, and RIGHT alone carries leftSpacer/rightSpacer.
# The two boxes are not mirror images; a port that shares one function between them
# will match LEFT and drift on RIGHT.

# PORTRAIT — the two-box split. boxWidth/boxHeight become the available size handed
# down to LEFT and RIGHT, so an error here moves every button on the screen.
PORTRAIT w=339 h=510 => x=49 boxw=166 boxh=249
PORTRAIT w=340 h=510 => x=50 boxw=170 boxh=255
PORTRAIT w=341 h=510 => x=50 boxw=170 boxh=255
PORTRAIT w=240 h=1080 => x=35 boxw=119 boxh=178
PORTRAIT w=300 h=1080 => x=44 boxw=149 boxh=224
PORTRAIT w=360 h=1440 => x=52 boxw=176 boxh=265
PORTRAIT w=432 h=1080 => x=63 boxw=214 boxh=321
PORTRAIT w=540 h=1200 => x=79 boxw=268 boxh=402
PORTRAIT w=360 h=600 => x=52 boxw=176 boxh=265
PORTRAIT w=360 h=800 => x=52 boxw=176 boxh=265
PORTRAIT w=540 h=700 => x=79 boxw=268 boxh=402
PORTRAIT w=540 h=900 => x=79 boxw=268 boxh=402
PORTRAIT w=720 h=1000 => x=105 boxw=357 boxh=535
PORTRAIT w=135 h=135 => x=19 boxw=64 boxh=96
PORTRAIT w=270 h=405 => x=39 boxw=132 boxh=198
PORTRAIT w=1080 h=1080 => x=158 boxw=537 boxh=805
PORTRAIT w=1080 h=2400 => x=158 boxw=537 boxh=805
PORTRAIT w=1440 h=3200 => x=211 boxw=717 boxh=1076
PORTRAIT w=2800 h=1400 => x=274 boxw=931 boxh=1397
PORTRAIT w=2400 h=1080 => x=211 boxw=717 boxh=1076
PORTRAIT w=100 h=100 => x=14 boxw=47 boxh=71
PORTRAIT w=200 h=100 => x=19 boxw=64 boxh=96
PORTRAIT w=720 h=100 => x=19 boxw=64 boxh=96

# PORTRAIT2 — the 135-unit grid. x is a FLOAT here (flooring would leave edge gaps
# on any resolution that is not a multiple of 135), and is floored at 1.0.
